children.label: ii
children.text: the greater of 50,000 or 10 percent of the population of the most populous city located within the nominated area, and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a rural area, 30,000.

children.heading: Distress
children.label: 2 Distress
children.text: The nominated area is one of pervasive poverty, unemployment, and general distress.

children.label: A
children.text: does not exceed 20 square miles if an urban area or 1,000 square miles if a rural area,

children.label: B
children.text: has a boundary which is continuous, or, except in the case of a rural area located in more than 1 State, consists of not more than 3 noncontiguous parcels,

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an urban area, is located entirely within no more than 2 contiguous States, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a rural area, is located entirely within no more than 3 contiguous States, and

children.label: D
children.text: does not include any portion of a central business district (as such term is used for purposes of the most recent Census of Retail Trade) unless the poverty rate for each population census tract in such district is not less than 35 percent (30 percent in the case of an enterprise community).

children.label: A
children.text: for each population census tract within the nominated area is not less than 20 percent,

children.label: B
children.text: for at least 90 percent of the population census tracts within the nominated area is not less than 25 percent, and

children.label: C
children.text: for at least 50 percent of the population census tracts within the nominated area is not less than 35 percent.

children.heading: Special rules relating to determination of poverty rate
children.label: b Special rules relating to determination of poverty rate

children.heading: Treatment of census tracts with small populations
children.label: 1 Treatment of census tracts with small populations
i
children.text: such tract shall be treated as having a poverty rate which meets the requirements of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (a)(4), but

children.label: ii
children.text: such tract shall be treated as having a zero poverty rate for purposes of applying subparagraph (C) thereof.

children.heading: Tracts with populations of less than 2,000
children.label: B Tracts with populations of less than 2,000
children.text: A population census tract with a population of less than 2,000 shall be treated as having a poverty rate which meets the requirements of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (a)(4) if more than 75 percent of such tract is zoned for commercial or industrial use.

children.heading: Discretion to adjust requirements for enterprise communities
children.label: 2 Discretion to adjust requirements for enterprise communities
A
children.text: The 20 percent threshold in subsection (a)(4)(A).

children.label: B
children.text: The 25 percent threshold in subsection (a)(4)(B).

children.label: C
children.text: The 35 percent threshold in subsection (a)(4)(C).

children.heading: Each noncontiguous area must satisfy poverty rate rule
children.label: 3 Each noncontiguous area must satisfy poverty rate rule
children.text: A nominated area may not include a noncontiguous parcel unless such parcel separately meets (subject to paragraphs (1) and (2)) the criteria set forth in subsection (a)(4).

children.heading: Areas not within census tracts
children.label: 4 Areas not within census tracts
children.text: In the case of an area which is not tracted for population census tracts, the equivalent county divisions (as defined by the Bureau of the Census for purposes of defining poverty areas) shall be used for purposes of determining poverty rates.
the effectiveness of the strategic plan submitted pursuant to section 1391(f)(2) and the assurances made pursuant to section 1391(e)(3), and
criteria specified by the appropriate Secretary.

children.heading: Special eligibility for nominated areas located in Alaska or Hawaii
children.label: d Special eligibility for nominated areas located in Alaska or Hawaii
children.text: A nominated area in Alaska or Hawaii shall be treated as meeting the requirements of paragraphs (2), (3), and (4) of subsection (a) if for each census tract or block group within such area 20 percent or more of the families have income which is 50 percent or less of the statewide median family income (as determined under section 143).
